title: HC Wainwright Cuts Intellia Therapeutics Price Target to $25 on Dilution
text: H.C. Wainwright reduced its price target for Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ: NTLA) to $25 from $30, citing shareholder dilution from a recent financing. The firm maintained its Buy rating despite the reduction. The stock currently trades at $13.99, valuing the company at $1.95 billion. According to the analysis, the company is quickly burning through cash with levered free cash flow of -$396 million over the last twelve months. However, the downgrade comes alongside significant clinical progress. The company's lonvo-z therapy delivered the first positive Phase 3 dataset for an in vivo gene-editing therapy, with an 87% attack reduction versus placebo. Trial data showed strong safety profiles and symptom relief, with a potential U.S. launch targeted for the first half of 2027. Despite the analyst reduction, Intellia's stock has surged 61% over the past six months. The company recently completed a $180 million equity offering at $10.75 per share, representing an 18.6% discount to recent closing prices.
